demographics.occupation: works in workplace diversity and inclusion; role involves a lot of educating and supporting colleagues

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: (Call placed by the patient's partner, patient speaking with difficulty) 'I have absolutely nothing left to give, but if I stop, everything falls apart. My body physically will not let me move anymore.'
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: They have been unable to get out of bed for about two days, can't stop crying, and feel completely wiped out. Their partner is worried and rang for help.

symptom_narrative.onset: a slow build-up over about six months of feeling more and more drained, a sharp worsening over the last couple of weeks, then a complete collapse over the last 48 hours
symptom_narrative.character: total exhaustion, heavy limbs, feels physically unable to move, crying on and off, can't think clearly or finish sentences
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: says they have nothing left at all — the worst they have ever felt
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the whole body, and a heavy, flattened feeling
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant; worse whenever they try to think about work or responsibilities
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far; only lying still and being away from work helps a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: thinking about work and everything they feel responsible for, and a wave of guilt whenever they try to rest
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: barely eating or drinking, feels hopeless and numb, heart sometimes feels like it is racing
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: has felt run-down and stretched for months but has never collapsed like this before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: cannot work, cannot do normal daily tasks, and has needed their partner's help just to drink water

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: works in a demanding, people-facing role that involves constantly educating and supporting others; has felt increasingly depleted and resentful and guilty about wanting to step back

objective_data_offered.value: partner reports they have been in bed for two days, needs help to drink water, is crying on and off and not eating
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: second-hand report from partner over the phone

objective_data_offered.value: appears very tired and unkempt, moves and speaks slowly, flat facial expression, poor eye contact, turns their head away when work is mentioned
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: subjective visual impression over a video link, not a formal examination

objective_data_offered.value: says their heart sometimes feels like it is racing
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient's own sensation; no measured heart rate available
